vulnerability.title: Configure opensc Smart Card Drivers
vulnerability.description: The OpenSC smart card tool can auto-detect smart card drivers; however, setting the smart card drivers in use by your organization helps to prevent users from using unauthorized smart cards. The default smart card driver for this profile is . To configure the OpenSC driver, edit the /etc/opensc.conf and add the following line into the file in the app default block, so it will look like: app default { ... card_drivers = ; }
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then var_smartcard_drivers='' OPENSC_TOOL="/usr/bin/opensc-tool" if [ -f "${OPENSC_TOOL}" ]; then ${OPENSC_TOOL} -S app:default:card_drivers:$var_smartcard_drivers fi else >&2 echo 'Remediation is not applicable, nothing was done' fi

triage_decision.reason: The remediation only edits /etc/opensc.conf to set the card_drivers parameter. This change does not affect system boot, SSH, or core authentication mechanisms and is reversible. Risk of lockout is minimal, making it safe to apply automatically.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Incorrect driver list could prevent legitimate smart card use

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need to propose plan, then review. We must edit /etc/opensc.conf to set card_drivers parameter within the app default block.

We need to read the file, see existing content. Then modify: find line within "app default {" block that defines card_drivers, maybe commented out, or add it if missing? The description says add in the block.

We will propose to use sed to add or replace line. Approach: Use sed to search for "app default {" block and ensure line "card_drivers = VAR" inside. Could replace existing "card_drivers =" line if present, else insert after the opening brace.

Simplify: Use awk? but only sed permissible.

Plan: 1. read_file /etc/opensc.conf. 2. Determine if line matching "^\s*card_drivers\s*=" exists inside block. If exists, uncomment and set value (maybe empty string?). var_smartcard_drivers likely empty ''. So set card_drivers = . Actually default recommended is ;? The example: card_drivers = ; maybe empty list. We'll set to empty (blank). So line: "card_drivers = " (nothing after equals). Use sed to replace: s/^\s*#\?\s*card_drivers\s*=.*$/    card_drivers = / inside block. But sed can't limit to block easily. Could replace globally, but risk other sections. Might be okay if only appears once.

We can use sed -i -e '/^\s*card_drivers\s*=/{s/^#\s*//;s/=.*/= /}' /etc/opensc.conf

If line doesn't exist, add after "app default {" line: use sed to insert after matching "app default {".

We'll implement: if grep -q "card_drivers" then replace, else add after.

We'll need to run commands to check existence.

Plan steps:

- read_file /etc/opensc.conf
- run_cmd "grep -q '^\\s*card_drivers' /etc/opensc.conf && echo yes || echo no"
- Based on output, either replace or insert.

But we cannot programmatically branch here; we just need to apply. We can just run two commands: first replace (sed) which does nothing if not present, then insert after block (sed) with -e '/app default {/,/}/{/card_drivers/!{/.*/a\    card_drivers = } }' maybe complicated.

Simplify: Append line inside block if missing: Use awk to detect but allowed only sed.

We can use sed to add after "app default {" if not already present: sed -i '/app default {/ { /card_drivers/! a\    card_drivers = }' /etc/opensc.conf

Thus two commands: first replace any existing lines, then ensure line exists.

Will plan accordingly.

Now produce plan description for review.
